name: KOSPI (Korea Composite Stock Index)
summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday; THREE-DAY window June 7 cutoff -> early June 10, June 8/9 runs empty; deltas vs June 7). **THE AI TAPE, NOT THE WAR — KOSPI 7,730.82 (+3.3%† 1D, +3.2% 1M, +39.7% 3M) REBOUNDED ~4.8% JUN 9 ON NVIDIA-KOREA AI DEALS AFTER AN >8% JUN 8 PLUNGE; A LEVERAGED SEMICONDUCTOR-SENTIMENT PROXY TRIPPING 5% CIRCUIT BREAKERS IN BOTH DIRECTIONS ON CONSECUTIVE DAYS (~5% crisis).** After a >8% chip-rout/war-jitters plunge June 8, KOSPI rebounded ~4.8% June 9 on NVIDIA-Korea AI-cooperation deals, President Lee's 'KOSPI is undervalued' remarks, and bargain-hunting; Samsung and SK Hynix each +~6%. Samsung + SK Hynix are now >50% of KOSPI market cap and ~72% of YTD gains — the index is a LEVERAGED semiconductor-sentiment proxy, which is why it tripped 5% circuit breakers in BOTH directions on consecutive days. Foreign investors net sellers; narrow breadth (~4% of components at highs). This is AI-rotation mechanics, ~5% crisis. DATA/TIMEZONE FLAG: the +3.3% at 7,730.82 most plausibly captures the June 9 rebound; treat the magnitude as noisy. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: KOSPI-below-7,500-sustained and the Samsung/SK-Hynix helium-halt trigger BOTH stay watching (rebounded above 7,500; helium overhang eased by secured inventory, no halt). PRIOR — Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session [May jobs + AI rout] -> 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange). **CONFIRMED + REFINED — THE 5-JUNE ASIA CRASH (PREVIEWED 06-04 AT -5.3%) REALISED AT -5.5% TO 8,160.59 WITH A CIRCUIT BREAKER; THE WORST KOSPI DAY IN 19 MONTHS. NOT A FRESH LEG — THE SAME BROADCOM/JOBS AI-VALUATION UNWIND, NOW QUANTIFIED.** The 5-June session realised the crack previewed on 06-04: KOSPI -5.5% to 8,160.59 (intraday -6.4%; KOSPI 200 futures -5% triggered a CIRCUIT BREAKER) — the worst day in 19 months after a ~109% six-month run. Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9% in the Asia session. Driver was the broad 5-June rates/AI risk-off: Broadcom's AI-guidance miss + a hot May jobs report (172K vs ~85K) -> sell-everything-rate-sensitive -> the crowded, leveraged North-Asia AI-memory trade unwound. Positioning evidence: foreign investors net-sold ~$22B Korean equities since May (SK Hynix alone ~-$12B); single-stock 2x leveraged ETFs -20%; cash rotating to the SpaceX IPO (Bloomberg). The concentration is the structure: Samsung + SK Hynix = 72% of the index's gains and ~54% of market cap — the index is two stocks. ~0% crisis, ~100% AI/leveraged-positioning; helium overhang eased (SK hynix secured inventory carry-forward). GRAPH HYGIENE: this is the SAME event previewed at -5.3% on 06-04 — confirm/refine, do NOT double-count as an additional leg. PORTFOLIO SIGNIFICANCE unchanged: KOSPI is the index whose strength has been CAUSING India's FII drain (capital_substitution edge) — a confirmed -5.5% circuit-breaker day is the hardest evidence yet the substitution trade can break and FII can reverse toward India; caveat that a violent crack is first a global risk-off. Watch SK Hynix/Samsung stabilising within 2-3 sessions. PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **THE SUPERCYCLE CRACKED — KOSPI -5.3% TO 8,177.25 (SAMSUNG -7%, SK HYNIX -9%, NEAR A CIRCUIT-BREAKER) ON BROADCOM'S AI-GUIDANCE MISS. THE FIRST VALUATION-DRIVEN CRACK, AND THE FIRST TIME THE FII-DRAIN FROM INDIA COULD REVERSE.** Broadcom's after-the-bell Q3 AI-revenue guide (+200% YoY to ~$16B) failed to justify its lofty valuation (AVGO -12.6%, SMH -4.4% live) -> the crowded North-Asia AI-memory trade gapped down: KOSPI -5.3% (Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9%, near a circuit-breaker), USD/KRW +0.6% (won weaker on the equity outflow). Korea co-drivers: CPI accelerated to 3.1% (a 2-yr high, domestic-rate-hike fear) + pure profit-take after +46% 3M; the helium overhang EASED today (SK hynix secured inventory, intel A11) — so this is a VALUATION crack (~0% crisis), NOT helium/input-supply. WHY IT MATTERS MOST FOR THE PORTFOLIO: KOSPI is the index whose strength has been CAUSING India's FII drain (the nifty-50 <-> semiconductors capital_substitution edge) — a -5.3% KOSPI is the FIRST hard evidence the substitution trade can break and the FII flow can reverse toward India (markets C1/C5/E1). CAVEAT: one bellwether's guide is not a confirmed regime change; a VIOLENT crack is first a global risk-off that hits everything (India included) before any rotation-back. Watch whether SK Hynix/Samsung stabilise within 2-3 sessions. PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers to 3 June morning). **KOSPI 8,801.49 (+0.1% 1D, +31.5% MoM, +52% 3M) — THE AI-MEMORY SUPERCYCLE STAYED DECOUPLED THROUGH THE FIRST CASUALTY DAY AND ABSORBED THE FII CAPITAL LEAVING INDIA.** On the cycle's first casualty-producing US-Iran day, KOSPI barely moved (+0.1%) and held near records while the inflation-regime metals selloff hit gold/silver/PGMs — confirming KOSPI is the AI-capex trade (~0-5% crisis), not a crisis asset, and the destination of the record FII outflow from India (Rs8,362.92cr on 2 June; the nifty-50 <-> semiconductors capital_substitution edge). Concentration risk unchanged: Samsung + SK Hynix ~half the index; the won is WEAKENING (USD/KRW Won1,528.79, +1.2% 1D, +5.3% 3M) even as KOSPI rips — FX outflow alongside equity inflow. The standing helium watch is the live tail: Korea fab inventories estimated to last 'until June' (now inside the buffer window) — the most reversible risk to the most crowded trade if a fab-halt materialises; none visible yet. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **RECORD HIGH ON SK HYNIX'S $1T MILESTONE — BUT THE INDEX IS TWO STOCKS. KOSPI +3.6% to a record 8,476.15 (+28.1% MoM, +46.3% 3M); SK Hynix jumped ~11% to join the $1 trillion club, rising as much as 5% intraday and triggering a 'sidecar' algo-halt. Goldman raised its KOSPI target to 9,000 and calls Korea its 'highest-conviction' market (+300% 2026 EPS growth). **Concentration is extreme: 82% of Korean listed stocks FELL over the past month; Samsung + SK Hynix alone are ~50% of the index by market cap — the narrowest possible breadth. The won is WEAKENING (USD/KRW Won1,507, +4.8% 3M) even as KOSPI rips — FX outflow alongside equity inflow.** This is the destination of capital leaving India (markets C3) — a structural AI-memory trade, ~0% crisis, reversible only on an idiosyncratic AI/memory-pricing crack, none visible.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **KOSPI 8,228.70 FRESH RECORD (+2.3% 1D, +27.1% MoM, +42.1% 3M, ~+90% YTD 2026) — THE AI/HBM MELT-UP CONTINUES, DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS. SK HYNIX HAS NOW JOINED SAMSUNG + MICRON IN THE $1 TRILLION CLUB. BOK RAISED ITS 2026 GROWTH FORECAST TO 2.6% FROM 2.0% ON CHIP EXPORTS. THIS IS CAPITAL ROTATION (money leaving expensive oil-exposed India, flowing into Korea/Taiwan AI), NOT A CRISIS MOVE (~95% NON-WAR).** (1) **Record + breadth collapse**: KOSPI printed a fresh record 8,228.70; intraday headlines flagged 'KOSPI tops 8,400 but only 1 in 10 stocks gains' — Samsung + SK Hynix are now >50% of KOSPI, an EXTREME concentration where the index IS the two chip names. (2) **Macro upgrade**: the Bank of Korea lifted its 2026 GDP growth forecast to 2.6% from 2.0%, explicitly on the chip-export boom. (3) **Rotation, not crisis**: the bid is the global AI/HBM build-out plus capital rotating OUT of expensive, oil-import-exposed India INTO Korea/Taiwan AI — the helms engineer is adding an india↔semiconductor capital-rotation edge. (4) **Currency confirms inflows**: USD/KRW firmed to ₩1,494 (won stronger on equity inflows), reversing the prior won-weakness divergence. (5) **Helium cross-check**: the war→helium→Korean-fab cascade is REAL on supply (~1/3 global helium offline, spot ~2x) but BUFFERED ~6 months by Korean stockpiles + long-term contracts → lagged, NOT biting; the helium→kospi edge is NOT activated by this AI rally. The live vulnerability remains CONCENTRATION + AI-capex re-pricing, independent of the war. PRIOR (May 27): Updated 2026-05-27 (~48h window; no brief ran May 26). **KOSPI ~8,048 (+2.5% 1D, +25.4% MoM, +28.9% 3M, +90% YTD 2026) — AT ALL-TIME HIGHS (intraday ~8,131, touched 8,450). THIS IS THE AI/HBM SUPER-CYCLE, DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS — it is why Asia indices sit at records on a week of US strikes inside Iran. SK Hynix +11% Wed crossed $1 TRILLION market cap; Samsung crossed $1T earlier in May; Micron $1T May 26. SAMSUNG + SK HYNIX ≈ HALF OF KOSPI CAP = the defining CONCENTRATION RISK — the index move IS those two chip names. The vulnerability is NOT the war: 'peak-out H2' warnings (Seoul Economic Daily) flag that 2 names ≈ half the index is fragile to any AI-capex re-pricing, INDEPENDENT of Iran. The helium cascade that could in theory hit Korean fabs is REAL on supply but BUFFERED ~6 months (stockpiles + long-term contracts) → lagged, not biting (see helium node). Do NOT model KOSPI as crisis-correlated (~95% non-war).** PRIOR (May 24): **KOSPI 7,847.71 FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (+0.4% 1D, +22.8% 1M, +34.2% 3M). MOST IMPORTANT SINGLE NUMBER IN THE DATA: KOSPI +34.2% IN 3 MONTHS — BIGGER THAN ANY CRISIS-ATTRIBUTABLE MOVE OF THE CYCLE. THIS IS NOT CRISIS-CORRELATED. IT IS THE AI CAPEX CYCLE. SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CROSSED $1 TRILLION MARKET CAP ON MAY 6 AFTER +15% SINGLE-DAY MOVE; SUSTAINED AI MEMORY/HBM DEMAND. SAMSUNG + SK HYNIX NOW ~42.2% OF KOSPI WEIGHTING — INDEX MOVE IS THE CHIP RALLY. THE INVESTOR'S MENTAL MODEL NEEDS TO INCORPORATE THAT THE DOMINANT 2026 MARKET STORY IS THE AI BUILD-OUT, NOT THE WEST ASIA CRISIS — EVEN THOUGH THE CRISIS DOMINATES DAILY ATTENTION.** **Primary chain (~90% AI-secular)**: Samsung $1T market cap May 6; SK Hynix HBM record on US hyperscaler demand; TSMC sympathy + Taiwan TAIEX +11.6% MoM. **Secondary contributor (~10% crisis-relief)**: helium supply fear had spiked April → modest unwinding as deal hope returned. **Assessment**: This is NOT a crisis-driven move. If the deal fails, KOSPI does not crash 22% — it might give back 3-5%. **Reversibility LOW for AI component; deal-related 10% is reversible**. **Risk**: concentration to 42% in two names is fragile. Any AI capex slowdown signal (Microsoft/Meta capex guide-down, NVIDIA inventory build) collapses the move regardless of crisis path. **USD/KRW ₩1,520.26** (+1.1% 1D, +2.7% MoM) — won weakening DESPITE KOSPI surge = capital-direction divergence (foreign FX outflow continues even as equity inflows). PRIOR (May 23): KOSPI 7,847.71 FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE — yfinance DATA HOLE RESOLVED. yfinance DATA HOLE RESOLVED. THE 1M MOVE WAS ALMOST ENTIRELY THURSDAY MAY 22 +~8% ON NVIDIA Q1 EARNINGS BEAT + SAMSUNG LABOR-STRIKE AVERSION + SK HYNIX +9.5%. AI-CYCLE REGIME IS THE DOMINANT NON-WAR DRIVER. USD/KRW ₩1,520.53 (+1.1% 1D, +2.7% MoM) — WON WEAKENING DESPITE KOSPI SURGE = CAPITAL-DIRECTION DIVERGENCE (FX OUTFLOW WHILE EQUITY INFLOWS).** **Primary chain (~75-90% AI-cycle)**: Nvidia Q1 earnings May 21: profit +200%+ YoY, revenue +85% YoY (CNBC, Invezz, IndexBox aggregator) → AI demand thesis affirmed at scale → chip-cycle bid revalidated; Samsung Electronics +7.5% May 22 after labor-union strike threat averted; SK Hynix +9.5% on HBM4/Nvidia partnership; KOSPI +~8% single-day. **Secondary chain (~25-30% crisis-linked)**: Helium-mitigation thesis — Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final qualification → June supply ramp positioned to handle helium-constrained Korean fab inventory window → 'helium crisis postponed but not solved' framing → near-term semi-supply continuity priced as positive. **Capital-direction divergence intelligence**: Korean retail/institutional may be participating in the equity rally; foreign capital is still net-outflow on macro-prudential grounds. **The 1M moves are NOT crisis-driven** and would persist if war resolved tomorrow. They would also persist if war escalated, unless escalation directly hit Asian semi-supply (Hormuz strike closing Korean helium imports for >60 days would be the breaker). **Reversibility low** — AI-cycle is multi-quarter regime, not daily event. The 1M is now structural. **JPMorgan ₩43T worst-case loss anchor stands** but probability-weighting has shifted further away from worst-case as HBM4-mitigation operationally validated. **Samsung Electronics + SK Hynix at 42.2% of KOSPI weight** = record concentration; AI-cycle dominance translates to KOSPI gains. PRIOR (May 22): **KOSPI yfinance DATA HOLE May 22 — INTEL REPORTS KOSPI 'SURGED OVER 8%' PER TRADINGKEY AGGREGATOR (REPORTED SINGLE-SOURCE); OPERATIONALLY INCONSISTENT WITH USD/KRW ₩1,517.04 +1.2% (WON WEAKENING). NEEDS VERIFICATION.** Tagging discipline: KOSPI 8% surge is REPORTED single-source; should not be encoded as CONFIRMED until cross-checked against KRX primary or Bloomberg. The won weakening alongside an alleged equity surge points to ASIAN equity AI-bid (Nvidia/HBM4) dominating capital flow direction while Korea-specific currency pressure persists. **Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final tests** with June supply ramp — this is the operational driver of any KOSPI rally narrative. **SK Hynix Q1 'sufficient helium inventory' + Korean chipmaker long-term US contracts**: helium-cascade Kornbluth thesis materially weakened, supports the AI-cycle decoupling read. **The triple-stressor stack (helium scarcity, Samsung 18-day strike May 21, capacity-zero) has been substantially relieved** by HBM4 mitigation news — the 18-day-strike risk overlay still exists, but the helium component is materially de-risked. **JPMorgan ₩43T worst-case loss anchor stands** but the probability-weighting has shifted away from worst-case. **Asian AI rally context**: SMH +2.1% 1D / +20.3% 1M / +40.4% 3M, Taiwan TAIEX +3.4% 1D / +10.0% 1M, Nikkei +3.1% 1D — KOSPI 8% surge fits the AI-cycle-decoupled-from-crisis pattern. **Verification next-cycle**: cross-check KRX primary close for May 22; if confirmed, helium → KOSPI cascade edge weight demotion warranted. PRIOR (May 19): **A10 KOSPI 7,541.48 +0.6% MONDAY — RALLYING ON SAMSUNG HBM4 ANNOUNCEMENT + 11 'EMPEROR STOCKS' RECORD; BEHAVIOURAL DIVERGENCE FROM KINETIC-PREP TAPE.** Monthly +23.8%, 3M +32.8%. SK Hynix trades at ₩1.836M (+0.93%); 11 'emperor stocks' (>₩1M) hit record. Brokerages (Eugene Investment, Mirae Asset) maintained ₩3.2M SK Hynix target prices and ₩10,000 KOSPI outlook. **The Monday rally is the cleanest demonstration this cycle that the Friday -6.1% break was Samsung-strike-and-NYT-prep priced in at the close, not a leading indicator of further drawdown** — Friday's move was the panic, not the trend. **Helium clock Day 3 of 14 (TrendForce two-week count); no new allocation notice from Samsung, SK Hynix, or TSMC through Monday morning IST.** Kornbluth thesis remains operative but the KOSPI tape is now actively contradicting the panic-asymmetry case for the next 5 trading sessions. **Markets dossier proposes 'kospi-helium-decoupling' analytical edge weakening**: demote helium → kospi edge weight from current ~7-8 toward 5-6 if KOSPI rally extends through Tuesday-Wednesday. **Samsung 18-day strike still scheduled May 21**. **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE PRICE IS FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSE 7,493.18 (-6.1% 1D, +25.6% 1M, +32.0% 3M).** Intraday signature: KOSPI hit 8,046.78 (first-ever breach of 8,000) early session, then closed -488 pts. **Foreigners net sold ₩5.6128 trillion (largest of cycle); 7th consecutive session of foreign selling.** Per markets dossier C5: crisis vs non-crisis split ~30% crisis / ~70% Samsung-specific (the strike is a domestic operational shock, not crisis-linked). **B5 SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE MAY 21 — 4 DAYS FROM START, NO NEW RESOLUTION DEVELOPMENT OVER THE WEEKEND.** Korea Herald / Tom's Hardware / Trading Key carried over from May 15-16 the unresolved union-management negotiation. Samsung labor union strike action remains scheduled May 21 to June 7 (18 days) with ~45,000 workers. KOSPI/SMH puts before May 21 remain asymmetric per yesterday's Kornbluth take. JPMorgan worst-case loss ₩43T anchor stands. **The Friday KOSPI -6.1% partial pre-validation of the trigger from yesterday's brief carries forward; the live test opens Wednesday May 20-21.** **Monday May 18 open is the first test of whether Friday's selloff was Samsung-strike priced-in or Iran-preparation-not-yet-priced-in** — the NYT preparation report adds a Korea-AI tail-risk overlay (helium clock Day 2 of 14 cumulates with Samsung strike). **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: Samsung component (~70%) — if union-management resolution before May 21, KOSPI bounces sharply; if strike begins, -10-15% further downside risk per JPMorgan ₩43T loss anchor. Crisis component (~30%) reverses on any genuine Iran-deal signal; further compounds if helium clock-expiry (Day 2 of 14) bites Q2. **PRIOR (May 16):** KOSPI -6.1% CONFIRMED CLOSE 7,493.18 (CNBC: 'South Korea's Kospi retreats from record high to drop over 6% as heavyweight tech names sell-off') — yesterday's intraday-roundtrip number now the official close.** SK Hynix capacity-zero framing and Samsung 18-day strike confirmation are the structural overhang for next week's open. **Markets dossier C3 triple-cause attribution**: (a) **Samsung 18-day strike CONFIRMED (~40%)** — NSEU confirmed May 21-June 7; JPMorgan ₩43T ($28.8B) worst-case loss; Samsung Electronics -8.6% Friday; Samsung + SK Hynix = 42.2% of KOSPI cap-weighted. (b) **Korea AI-Profit-Redistribution Proposal (~30%)** — Kim Yongbeom (Korean presidential office policy chief) May 11-12 Facebook proposal 'AI national dividend' using AI-boom-tax revenue; structural fear is windfall-tax precedent. (c) **Foreign Capital Outflow Cascade (~30%)** — foreign net sell ₩1.8T ($1.21B) on May 15 alone; 7th consecutive session; cumulative ₩5.6T over 7 sessions; USD/KRW ₩1,497.76 broke 1,500 intraday — FX-hedged foreign capital faces compounding losses. **NOT crisis-driven**: Iran rhetoric ~5%; Korea largely insulated from Brent direct; helium tail risk structural (~6-week buffer), not Friday-specific. **Crisis component: ~25%; 75% is domestic Korean dynamics.** **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: JPMorgan ₩43T worst-case structural; if strike resolves quickly OR Samsung resumes negotiations May 19-20, sharp bounce possible; full 18-day run = another -10-15% downside. **The 'Samsung 18-day strike → KOSPI/SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start' trigger added yesterday is partially pre-validated by Thursday's -6.1% KOSPI; the next 5 sessions are now the live test window.** PRIOR (May 15): **C1 KOSPI -6.1% TO 7,493.20 — THE SINGLE LARGEST EQUITY MOVE OF THE CYCLE**: KOSPI opened May 15 at 7,951, briefly broke 8,000 for the first time ever (intraday high 8,046.78), then reversed violently to close 7,493.20 — intraday roundtrip of ~7%, not a gap-down. **This is a forced unwinding off an extreme long, not a fundamental regime change.** **Primary chain (crisis-linked, ~2.4%)**: Trump told media overnight he 'would no longer tolerate' Iran + 'would no longer be patient,' floated entombing/removing Iran's enriched uranium stockpile (contradicting Trump-Xi joint statement diplomatic tone) → US after-hours futures rolled over → Seoul opened weak → helium overhang re-engaged (Korea sources 64.7% from Qatar; Samsung HeRS recovery and SK Hynix 6mo claim partially offsetting but May 16-23 window remains) → oil-import-bill pressure on USD/KRW (₩1,500.68, +0.7% — first breach of 1,500 psychological level) → foreign safe-haven USD bid. **Secondary chain (Samsung strike-specific, ~2.1%)**: Samsung union confirmed May 21-June 7 18-day strike (45,000+ workers) after talks broke down May 12-13; Industry Minister warned 100 trillion won damage; Samsung pre-emptively cut wafer input; Samsung Electronics intraday -5.4% from prior-day all-time high 296,000 won; sector-wide drag on KOSPI's largest weighting. **Tertiary chain (positioning, ~1.5%)**: Samsung + SK Hynix at 42.2% of KOSPI weight — record concentration; foreigners already net sold ₩27 trillion over prior 7 sessions; on May 15 alone foreigners dumped ₩4.4 trillion ($2.94B) by 2pm; +25.6% MoM rally was a coiled spring at 8,000 milestone. **ASSESSMENT: MOSTLY REVERSIBLE.** Samsung settles → ~35% recovers fast (May 13 precedent: Samsung recovered $66B intraday wipeout same-day on Seoul intervention). Iran de-escalates → ~40% recovers (helium overhang lifts, oil drops, won strengthens). Foreign positioning ~25% may not reverse quickly (concentration risk is structural). KB Securities raised 2026 target to 10,500 on May 14. **JPMorgan 12-month bull-case still 10,000 (current 7,493 = +33% upside if rally re-engages); Goldman Sachs reiterates 'top conviction' on Korean equities with 9,000 12m target.** **Helium 2-week-clock now at Day 74 since Ras Laffan halt; helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23** — KOSPI most exposed major index; supply-side stack compounding (helium + 18-day Samsung strike May 21 + SK Hynix 'essentially zero' available capacity). **The +36.7% MoM rally hit Trump 'no longer patient' overnight + confirmed Samsung 18-day strike + foreign profit-taking + intraday Samsung Electronics -5.4% as it printed all-time high then reversed.** AI super-cycle dominant: **Samsung Electronics +111% YTD and SK Hynix +144% YTD = 42.2% of KOSPI**; Korea chip exports +150% YoY first 10 days May to record $8.54B/10 days. **JPMorgan raised KOSPI 12-month bull-case target to 10,000 (current ~7,942; +26% upside implied); Goldman Sachs reiterates 'top conviction' on Korean equities with 9,000 12m target.** USD/KRW ₩1,489.81 (-0.2% 1D) — won steadying as KOSPI rally absorbs FX outflow. **Helium 2-week-clock at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt; helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23** — KOSPI most exposed major index. **Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises** (-15-25% snap-back to gut KOSPI +36.7% MoM rally). Currently pricing zero supply-chain risk while supply-chain risk is most quantifiable real-world risk in entire complex. A13 HMM Namu situation unchanged — South Korean attribution 'various actors' hedge carries forward (no new amplification from US/Israeli press today). PRIOR (May 13): FM Cho Hyun's 'too early' on HMM Namu attribution + PPP opposition pressure DEFUSES immediate 'Korea forced to name Iran' diplomatic spiral. The 'double-tap drone' forensic confirmation is OPERATIONAL fact; the diplomatic implication is HEDGED. Korean helium-fab buffer ~4 weeks remains structural ceiling — Kornbluth 'tsunami a thousand miles offshore' thesis intact one more session. KOSPI -1.7% May 12 selloff in yesterday's brief is now BETTER-UNDERSTOOD AS PARTIAL REPRICING OF DIPLOMATIC-FRICTION RISK rather than definitive Iran-attribution event.** USD/KRW ₩1,489.12 (+1.0% 1D = DISCONNECT — currency says capital outflow, equity says relief). If both reverse together, regime change. **Asia split: KOSPI +2.8% relief vs SMH -2.6% / TAIEX -1.3% (semis cooled on AI-megacap profit-take, rotation into industrial-cyclicals Cu +2.5%).** Crisis-linked share: ~35% (relief), ~65% AI semis bid. Brent retrace + Trump-Xi summit-trade hopes = Asia ex-China-Japan reflation bid; KOSPI gets cleanest relief. **Watch USD/KRW: if both KOSPI bounce AND USD/KRW reverse together, regime change. Iran-named-attribution within 14 days would reprice helium tail risk in a hurry.** Reversibility: KOSPI bounce reverses on Iran-named-attribution; SMH unwind reverses on AI-capex catalyst return (NVIDIA earnings May 28). ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): KOSPI is primary Korean equity benchmark; persistent index for Korean memory-fab + AI-semis + helium cascade. **KOSPI 7,690.59 May 12 (-1.7% 1D — first profit-taking session of cycle; +33.1% 1M / +39.3% 3M / +78% YTD into May 11).** The largest 30-day move in any major-market index in recent memory. **Crisis-linked cascade path: war → Qatar Ras Laffan offline → helium constraint → 4-week countdown to Korean fab buffer exhaustion (mid-June) → Samsung/SK Hynix production-halt risk.** **B9 HMM Namu forensics public May 11: Korean joint investigation confirmed strike by 2 unidentified aerial objects ~1 min apart at stern; mine + torpedo RULED OUT; SEOUL STOPPED SHORT OF PUBLICLY BLAMING IRAN — creating diplomatic friction with Trump.** Korea has gone from 'passive helium victim' to 'active diplomatic actor.' USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 (+1.9% 1D) on Korea-Iran-attribution friction + capital outflow. Crisis-linked share: ~40% (helium tail + HMM Namu + diplomatic friction); secular ~60% (AI capex super-cycle dominating Samsung +111% YTD / SK Hynix +144% YTD). **STILL MOST REVERSIBLE POSITION IN WORLD if helium buffers deplete past mid-June.** Watch level: KOSPI 7,500 hold within 5 sessions confirms Korea AI-bid intact despite diplomatic friction.

current.delta_1d: +0.1% -- consolidated after Monday's +6.8% record rip; AI supercycle intact (+16.5% 1M).
current.driver: TWO LEGS: ~40% US-Iran de-escalation relief, ~60% the AI/memory valuation bounce (post the 10 Jun unwind). High-vol two-way tape; the helium overhang and the IDC -13% smartphone demand cut remain the structural bears underneath.
current.positioning_evidence: Samsung + SK Hynix = >50% of KOSPI market cap and ~72% of YTD gains — the index is two stocks. Foreign investors net sellers; narrow breadth (~4% of components at highs).
current.portfolio_significance: KOSPI is the index whose strength has been CAUSING India's FII drain (capital_substitution edge). The consecutive-day circuit breakers in both directions show the substitution trade is now two-sided and volatile, but the Jun 9 rebound (NVIDIA-Korea AI deals) reasserts the AI bid — no confirmed sustained reversal toward India. Caveat: a violent crack is first a global risk-off.

price_snapshots.trigger: SK Hynix joined the $1 trillion club (~+11%), intraday +5% triggered a sidecar algo-halt; HBM/AI-memory supercycle + Goldman 'highest-conviction' / target 9,000.

price_snapshots.trigger: All-time high; +25.4% MoM / +90% YTD 2026 AI super-cycle run; SK Hynix crosses $1T
price_snapshots.delta_context: Up from ~6,400 a month prior; the largest sustained equity move of 2026, decoupled from the crisis. Samsung + SK Hynix ≈ half of index cap — the move is the chip rally.

active_triggers.condition: KOSPI breaks below 7,500 sustained 3 sessions — confirms AI-bid cracking under diplomatic friction
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS watching. KOSPI 7,730.82 (Jun 9) rebounded ~4.8% ABOVE 7,500 after a brief Jun 8 plunge; no 3-session sustained break below 7,500. The Jun 9 NVIDIA-Korea-AI-deal rebound reasserts the AI bid. Not triggered.

active_triggers.condition: Samsung or SK hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage by mid-June
active_triggers.note: Inherits from helium node — most time-sensitive trigger in graph; ~4 weeks remaining.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS watching. Helium near-term cliff remains DEFUSED by SK Hynix's secured inventory (carry-forward); no production halt announced. Samsung/SK Hynix each +~6% Jun 9 was an AI-rotation rebound, not an input-supply event.

top_edges.context: 2026-06-10: the concentration ran in BOTH directions on consecutive days — KOSPI plunged >8% Jun 8 (chip rout + war jitters) then rebounded ~4.8% Jun 9 on NVIDIA-Korea AI deals + Lee 'undervalued', Samsung/SK Hynix each +~6%; both moves tripped 5% circuit breakers. Confirms KOSPI as a leveraged semiconductor-sentiment proxy (Samsung+SK Hynix >50% of cap, ~72% of YTD gains). Weight held at cap 10.0; activation_count bumped to log the bidirectional swing.

recent_signals.headline: N-Asia AI complex consolidated after Monday's record rip -- KOSPI +0.1%, Nikkei +0.9% (still +15-16% 1M). The AI supercycle remains the dominant non-war force and an active capital competitor to India (still +15% 1M vs Nifty ~+2%). ~20% crisis-linked. CONFIRMED.

North-Asia AI complex RIP: KOSPI +6.8% (record ~8,874 intraday), Nikkei +4.9% (record), TAIEX +3.2%, SMH +4.4%. PRIMARILY the AI semiconductor supercycle — SK Hynix +11% on AI/HBM demand, strong Korean export data — plus SpaceX IPO liquidity; the Iran framework is only a ~30% tailwind. KEY: this is an active capital COMPETITOR to India — N-Asia +3-7% vs Nifty +1% on the same risk-on day. ~30% crisis-linked. CONFIRMED (yfinance).

KOSPI +4.6% (TAIEX +2.4%, Nikkei +2.8%) — RISK-ON RELIEF + AI/CHIP REBOUND, a sharp two-way whipsaw from the 10 Jun circuit-breaker DOWN week. ~40% de-escalation relief, ~60% the AI/memory bounce (SMH +1.7%, +8.3% 1M). The North-Asia tape is high-vol and headline-driven; the structural helium overhang persists underneath.

KOSPI -4.5% (Samsung -6.1%, SK Hynix -7.5%), TAIEX -3.3%, Nikkei -1.9% -- a circuit-breaker week with VKOSPI at a RECORD high. Two braided drivers: ~60% the post-Broadcom AI/memory valuation unwind (leg 3; IDC's record -13% 2026 smartphone forecast hits memory directly) + ~40% crisis-derived (US CPI 4.2% -> Fed-hike repricing -> duration-tech compression + USD/KRW W1,530 leveraged-Korea unwind). Leg one of the India capital-substitution trigger (North-Asia AI correction >10% from peak) is now UNAMBIGUOUSLY met.

**THE AI TAPE, NOT THE WAR — KOSPI 7,730.82 (+3.3%† 1D, +3.2% 1M, +39.7% 3M) REBOUNDED ~4.8% JUN 9 ON NVIDIA-KOREA AI DEALS AFTER AN >8% JUN 8 PLUNGE; 5% CIRCUIT BREAKERS IN BOTH DIRECTIONS ON CONSECUTIVE DAYS (~5% crisis).** After an >8% plunge June 8 (chip rout + war jitters), KOSPI rebounded ~4.8% June 9 on NVIDIA-Korea AI-cooperation deals, President Lee's 'KOSPI is undervalued' remarks, and bargain-hunting; Samsung and SK Hynix each +~6%. Samsung + SK Hynix are now >50% of KOSPI market cap and ~72% of YTD gains — the index is a LEVERAGED semiconductor-sentiment proxy, which is why it tripped 5% circuit breakers in BOTH directions on consecutive days. Foreign investors net sellers; narrow breadth (~4% of components at highs). DATA/TIMEZONE FLAG: the +3.3% at 7,730.82 most plausibly captures the June 9 rebound; treat magnitude as noisy. Triggers stay watching (rebounded above 7,500; helium overhang eased, no halt).

recent_signals.headline: **CONFIRMED/REFINED — THE 5-JUNE CRASH REALISED AT -5.5% TO 8,160.59 WITH A CIRCUIT BREAKER; WORST KOSPI DAY IN 19 MONTHS. NOT A FRESH LEG — THE SAME BROADCOM/JOBS AI-VALUATION UNWIND PREVIEWED 06-04 AT -5.3%, NOW QUANTIFIED.** The 5-June session realised the crack previewed on 06-04: KOSPI -5.5% to 8,160.59 (intraday -6.4%; KOSPI 200 futures -5% triggered a CIRCUIT BREAKER) — the worst day in 19 months after a ~109% six-month run; Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9% in the Asia session. Driver: the broad 5-June rates/AI risk-off — Broadcom's AI-guidance miss + a hot May jobs report (172K vs ~85K) -> sell-everything-rate-sensitive -> the crowded, leveraged North-Asia AI-memory trade unwound. Positioning: foreign investors net-sold ~$22B Korean equities since May (SK Hynix alone ~-$12B); single-stock 2x leveraged ETFs -20%; cash rotating to the SpaceX IPO (Bloomberg). Concentration: Samsung + SK Hynix = 72% of index gains, ~54% of market cap — the index is two stocks. ~0% crisis, ~100% AI/leveraged-positioning; helium overhang eased. GRAPH HYGIENE: same event previewed at -5.3% — confirm/refine, do NOT double-count. The capital_substitution-reversal read strengthens; caveat that a violent crack is first a global risk-off.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-06-06 (KOSPI -5.5% to 8,160.59, intraday -6.4%, KOSPI 200 futures -5% circuit breaker, worst day in 19 months; Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9%; foreign net-sell ~$22B since May, SK Hynix ~-$12B; 2x leveraged ETFs -20%; Samsung+SK Hynix 72% of gains / ~54% of cap)
Bloomberg (cash rotating to SpaceX IPO); cross-asset: same Broadcom/May-jobs rates/AI risk-off

recent_signals.headline: **THE SUPERCYCLE CRACKED — KOSPI -5.3% TO 8,177.25 (SAMSUNG -7%, SK HYNIX -9%) ON BROADCOM'S AI-GUIDANCE MISS. THE FIRST VALUATION CRACK, AND THE FIRST TIME THE FII-DRAIN FROM INDIA COULD REVERSE.** Broadcom's after-the-bell Q3 AI-revenue guide failed to justify its lofty valuation (AVGO -12.6%, SMH -4.4% live) -> the crowded North-Asia AI-memory trade gapped down: KOSPI -5.3% (Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9%, near a circuit-breaker), USD/KRW +0.6% (won weaker). Korea co-drivers: CPI 3.1% (2-yr high) + profit-take after +46% 3M; helium overhang eased today (SK hynix secured inventory). VALUATION crack, ~0% crisis, NOT helium. WHY IT MATTERS: KOSPI is the index whose strength has been CAUSING India's FII drain (capital_substitution edge) — a -5.3% KOSPI is the first hard evidence the substitution trade can break and FII can reverse toward India (markets C1/C5/E1). Caveat: a violent crack is first a global risk-off; watch SK Hynix/Samsung stabilising within 2-3 sessions.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-06-04 C1/C5/E1 (KOSPI -5.3% / Samsung -7% / SK Hynix -9% on Broadcom; valuation not helium; first session capital_substitution could reverse)
Intel 2026-06-04 A11 (SK hynix secured helium inventory); CNBC/TradingKey (KOSPI/Samsung/SK Hynix)
recent_signals.verification: KOSPI -5.3% / Samsung -7% / SK Hynix -9% tape = CONFIRMED (market data); Broadcom guide miss driver = CONFIRMED (AVGO earnings); ~0% crisis / VALUATION attribution + capital_substitution potential reversal = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS (markets C1/E1). No trigger to active — the AI cycle's first crack, not a confirmed regime change.

recent_signals.headline: **RECORD ON SK HYNIX'S $1T MILESTONE — BUT THE INDEX IS TWO STOCKS.** KOSPI +3.6% to a record 8,476 (+28% MoM, +46% 3M); SK Hynix +~11% to join the $1T club, intraday +5% triggering a sidecar halt; Goldman target 9,000, Korea 'highest-conviction.' 82% of Korean stocks fell over the past month; Samsung+SK Hynix = ~50% of the index (narrowest breadth). Won weakening (Won1,507, +4.8% 3M) even as KOSPI rips. The destination of capital leaving India; reversible only on an AI/memory crack.
sources: The Star / Seoul Economic Daily; Goldman
recent_signals.verification: KOSPI +3.6% record / SK Hynix $1T = CONFIRMED; 82%-fell + 2-stock concentration = CONFIRMED (Seoul Economic Daily).

recent_signals.headline: **KOSPI 8,228.70 FRESH RECORD (+2.3% 1D, +27.1% MoM, +42.1% 3M, ~+90% YTD 2026) — AI/HBM MELT-UP, CAPITAL ROTATION NOT CRISIS. SK HYNIX JOINS SAMSUNG + MICRON IN THE $1T CLUB; BOK RAISES 2026 GROWTH TO 2.6% ON CHIP EXPORTS; SAMSUNG + SK HYNIX NOW >50% OF KOSPI.** (1) **Record + breadth collapse**: KOSPI hit a fresh record 8,228.70; intraday headlines noted 'KOSPI tops 8,400 but only 1 in 10 stocks gains' — Samsung + SK Hynix are now >50% of KOSPI cap, an extreme concentration where the index move IS the two chip names. (2) **The $1T club fills out**: SK Hynix has now joined Samsung and Micron above $1 trillion market cap on the HBM/AI semis melt-up. (3) **Macro upgrade**: the Bank of Korea raised its 2026 GDP growth forecast to 2.6% (from 2.0%), explicitly citing the chip-export boom. (4) **Rotation, not crisis (~95% non-war)**: this is capital rotating OUT of expensive, oil-import-exposed India INTO Korea/Taiwan AI plus the global AI/HBM build-out — NOT a West Asia crisis cascade. The won FIRMED to USD/KRW ₩1,494 on equity inflows, reversing the prior won-weakness divergence. (5) **Helium cross-check**: the war→helium→fab cascade is REAL on supply (~1/3 global helium offline, spot ~2x) but BUFFERED ~6 months by Korean stockpiles + long-term contracts → lagged, NOT biting; the helium→kospi edge is NOT activated. The live vulnerability is concentration + AI-capex re-pricing, independent of the war.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-28 A/B/C4/E (KOSPI 8,228.70 +2.3% 1D / +27.1% MoM / +42.1% 3M; SK Hynix joins $1T club; BOK 2026 growth 2.6% from 2.0%; Samsung+SK Hynix >50% of KOSPI; USD/KRW ₩1,494; rotation-not-crisis)
web (SK Hynix joins Samsung/Micron $1T club; BOK growth forecast upgrade to 2.6%; 'KOSPI tops 8,400 but only 1 in 10 stocks gains' — concentration)
recent_signals.verification: KOSPI 8,228.70 +2.3% 1D / +27.1% MoM / +42.1% 3M = CONFIRMED (market data, web-verified); SK Hynix $1T / BOK 2.6% growth forecast / breadth-collapse headline = REPORTED (web, multi-source); capital-rotation (India→Korea/Taiwan) + ~95% non-crisis read = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS (markets dossier C4/E); helium→kospi NOT activated = deliberate graph-hygiene (cascade buffered/lagged, AI rally non-crisis).

recent_signals.headline: **KOSPI ~8,048 AT ALL-TIME HIGHS (+2.5% 1D, +25.4% MoM, +28.9% 3M, +90% YTD 2026) — AI/HBM SUPER-CYCLE, DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS. SK HYNIX +11% WED CROSSES $1 TRILLION; SAMSUNG + SK HYNIX ≈ HALF OF KOSPI CAP = CONCENTRATION RISK.** (1) **The decoupling**: KOSPI rallied to all-time highs (intraday ~8,131, touched 8,450) on a week of US strikes inside Iran — the cleanest evidence that the index is riding the AI/HBM build-out, NOT the West Asia crisis. Samsung crossed $1T earlier in May, Micron $1T May 26, SK Hynix +11% Wed to cross $1T. (2) **Concentration is the live risk, NOT the war**: Samsung + SK Hynix ≈ half of KOSPI cap means the index move IS the chip rally — 'peak-out H2' warnings (Seoul Economic Daily) flag that 2 names ≈ half the index is fragile to any AI-capex re-pricing, independent of Iran. (3) **Helium cross-check**: the war→helium→Korean-fab cascade is REAL on supply (~1/3 global helium offline, spot +40-100%) but BUFFERED ~6 months by stockpiles + long-term contracts → lagged, NOT biting; the helium→kospi edge is NOT activated by this AI rally (~95% non-crisis). (4) **Graph hygiene**: do NOT read this as cascade-from-Iran; the move would have happened on the AI tape regardless of the deal track.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-27 C3 (KOSPI all-time high ~8,131 / intraday 8,450 / +90% YTD; SK Hynix +11% → $1T; Samsung+SK Hynix ≈ half of KOSPI cap; peak-out H2 concentration risk; decoupled, ~95% non-crisis)

recent_signals.headline: **KOSPI -3.3% TO 7,271.66 — IDIOSYNCRATIC, NOT IRAN-LINKED (~85% of move): SAMSUNG LABOR STRIKE RISK + 'AI PROFIT REDISTRIBUTION' POLICY + 8TH CONSECUTIVE DAY OF FOREIGN SELLING (>₩1T MAY 19).** Defense subsector ONLY green on the day. **CLEAN EXAMPLE OF A NON-CRISIS MARKET MOVE** — the investor should NOT read the KOSPI move as cascade-from-Iran. The helium-supply thesis (Kornbluth) is INDEPENDENT and structurally intact, but it is not what drove Tuesday's -3.3%. Background contributors (~15%): helium-clock Day 4 of 14 + Samsung strike date May 21 + Micron-style memory read-through. **Reversibility**: tomorrow's tape inverts if Samsung-labor settles; the 'AI profit redistribution' overhang persists until government clarifies. **For graph hygiene**: the helium → kospi edge should NOT be strengthened today (idiosyncratic dominant); the samsung-labor-strike-risk → kospi connection should be added as a new edge so the graph doesn't double-count it as crisis cascade.
recent_signals.verification: KOSPI -3.3% = CONFIRMED operational tape; Samsung labor strike risk = REPORTED; AI profit redistribution policy proposal = REPORTED; foreign selling 8th consecutive session = CONFIRMED; defense subsector only green = CONFIRMED (Seoul Economic Daily).

recent_signals.headline: **C4 KOSPI 7,941.95 (+1.2% 1D, +36.7% 1M, +39.9% 3M) — REBOUND CONTINUES** into Trump-Xi summit narrative. AI super-cycle dominant: Samsung +111% YTD / SK Hynix +144% YTD = 42.2% of KOSPI; chip exports +150% YoY first 10 days May to $8.54B/10 days. **JPMorgan raised KOSPI 12-month bull-case target to 10,000** (current +26% upside implied); **Goldman Sachs 'top conviction' with 9,000 12m target.** USD/KRW ₩1,489.81 (-0.2% 1D) won steadying as KOSPI rally absorbs FX outflow. **Helium 2-week-clock at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2; helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23** — KOSPI most exposed major index. **Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises** (-15-25% snap-back). Currently pricing zero supply-chain risk while supply-chain risk is most quantifiable real-world risk in entire complex. A13 HMM Namu situation unchanged (FM Cho 'various actors' hedge carries forward).

recent_signals.headline: C4 KOSPI 7,855.47 +2.8% 1D = REBOUND after May 12 -1.7% sell-off. FM Cho Hyun's 'too early' on HMM Namu attribution + PPP opposition pressure DEFUSES immediate 'Korea forced to name Iran' diplomatic spiral. 'Double-tap drone' forensic confirmation is OPERATIONAL fact; diplomatic implication is HEDGED. Korean helium-fab buffer ~4 weeks remains structural ceiling. KOSPI -1.7% May 12 is now BETTER-UNDERSTOOD AS PARTIAL REPRICING OF DIPLOMATIC-FRICTION RISK rather than definitive Iran-attribution event. USD/KRW ₩1,489.12 (+1.0% 1D) = DISCONNECT — currency says capital outflow, equity says relief. SMH -2.6% / TAIEX -1.3% break synchronicity with KOSPI = rotation from AI-megacap → industrial-cyclical (Cu +2.5%, Ag +2.6%). Crisis-linked ~35% (relief on diplomatic hedge); secular ~65% (AI semis bid intact one more session).
sources: Korea Herald FM Cho remarks + 'Fired upon in Hormuz' editorial
Seoul Economic Daily 'Presidential Office Condemns HMM Namu'
UPI + Washington Times (HMM Namu external strike confirmed)
recent_signals.verification: KOSPI +2.8% 1D = CONFIRMED tape; HMM Namu external strike + FM Cho 'too early' = CONFIRMED (Seoul official briefing + Korea Herald primary); PPP opposition pressure = CONFIRMED (Korean political reporting).

recent_signals.headline: KOSPI -1.7% 1D = FIRST PROFIT-TAKING SESSION OF CYCLE after extraordinary +33.1% 1M / +39.3% 3M / +78% YTD into May 11 — largest 30-day move in any major-market index in recent memory. Drivers: (1) HMM Namu forensics public May 11 — Seoul stopped short of publicly blaming Iran, creating diplomatic friction with Trump; (2) USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 +1.9% 1D on capital outflow; (3) mechanical profit-taking in a vertical market. Korea has gone from passive helium victim to active diplomatic actor. SMH $576.31 +1.7% 1D / +31.9% 1M; AI infrastructure CapEx demand from Microsoft/Meta/Amazon unbroken. Helium tail-risk timeline: Korean fab buffer ~4 weeks remaining to mid-June exhaustion. Tier-1 chipmaker production-halt falsification still HOLDS but window contracting.
sources: Seoul official briefing + Bloomberg + Insurance Journal + Korea Herald (HMM Namu forensics)
